editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Hannah Murphy to an unknown recipient. In the letter Murphy pleads with the recipient to get her children back. She tells of how her son is sick and she wishes for him to let out so that she can care for him. Murphy describes how her husband would also be grateful for this and wishes the recipient all the best luck.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3922

correspAction.placeName: 14 Francis Street, Tralee, County Kerry, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Refer to the
Chief Secretary
Decision of
22nd Inst
EOF
1/2/16 Tralee Jan 1916 Dear Sir I am writing
this note to let me no or can
you give me a desided answere
wheather they children are to come out
of the home or not as I would
like to no if you would leve
out the three boys same
Dear Sir let out my son daniel
Mc Carthy he is not two good
in health also their is a little
braking out in his face
send me a note and I will
get him out my self and my
husband will be very
thankful to you if you
will leave him out to me
as I can look after him
when he is not two strong
May God send you every
good Luck write to me
and let me no sir your faithfully hannah
Murphy
address
Mrs Hannah Murphy No 14 Francis Street
Tralee, Co. Kerry
Ireland My son is in Tralee home
St Joseph Christian Bros.
Industrial School
Tralee
